subject: Mathematics
title: Descartes: Where Algebra Meets Geometry
description: A quick, practical guide to Descartes: Where Algebra Meets Geometry.

hook.text: Legend says Descartes invented coordinate geometry while watching a fly crawl across his ceiling.

buildup.text: He placed two number lines at right angles, creating the x-y plane we use today.

discovery.text: Suddenly, every geometric shape could be written as an equation, and every equation could be graphed.

twist.text: He published his math as an appendix to a philosophy book. It almost went unnoticed.

climax.text: Cartesian coordinates united algebra and geometry—the most productive merger in math history.

punchline.text: A fly on the ceiling changed mathematics forever.

quiz.question: What did Descartes' coordinate system unite?
